question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: FindByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user flood FindByHash() with arbitrarily long hash strings (no length validation at line 136) to exhaust memory across multiple Rosetta mirror node instances, causing >=30% of processing nodes to crash or become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: FindByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of concurrent FindByHash() requests with unique non-existent hashes, each triggering the expensive correlated subquery in selectByHashWithIndex (line 34-45), exhausting the PostgreSQL connection pool and causing >=30% of mirror node instances to fail to serve requests?
